findings.evidence: `TubeData` adds `U_slsc`, `V_pathConn`, `V_left_subset`, and `V_right_subset` at lines 329-337, but the proof of `TubeData.isOpen` uses only `T.U_open` at line 504 and `T.V_open` at line 516; the based-path analogue is already stated at the raw-family level in `isOpen_refined_tubeNeighborhood` lines 573-577.
findings.fix: Prove a raw-family theorem first, e.g. for `{γ' | (∀ i s, ... → γ' s ∈ U i) ∧ ∀ j, γ' (part.t j) ∈ V j}` from `(∀ i, IsOpen (U i))` and `(∀ j, IsOpen (V j))`, then derive `TubeData.isOpen` as the specialization.
findings.issue: `TubeData.isOpen` requires a full `TubeData X n`, including `U_slsc`, `V_pathConn`, and the vertex-subset fields, for a compact-open topology statement that only depends on `U_open` and `V_open`.

summary: One public openness theorem is stated at the full SLSC tube-data level even though the result only needs open segment and vertex families.
